seo.title: Tax Tribunal Lawyer Lahore | ATIR Specialist Appeals
seo.description: Tax tribunal lawyer in Lahore. ATIR appeals for income tax, sales tax, and Federal Excise Duty. Specialist representation at Appellate Tribunal Inland Revenue

hero.intro: Tax tribunal lawyer in Lahore for ATIR appeals. We represent taxpayers in income tax, sales tax, and Federal Excise Duty disputes at the Appellate Tribunal Inland Revenue.

bodyMarkdown:
Reviewed by Bilal Saeed, Advocate (Punjab Bar Council). Last updated May 2026.

The Appellate Tribunal Inland Revenue (ATIR) is the second appellate forum for tax disputes in Pakistan. A tax tribunal lawyer in Lahore drafting an appeal must understand ATIR's jurisdiction, master the procedural rules, and know how to frame grounds of appeal that survive admission and lead to wins. After the Commissioner Inland Revenue (Appeals) rejects a taxpayer's initial appeal, ATIR is the next step. It is the final stage where both facts and law can be argued before the case narrows to questions of law alone at the Lahore High Court.



### ATIR Appeal Timeline and Process Overview

| Stage | Duration | What Happens |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| Filing | Within 60 days of Commissioner order | Submit memorandum, supporting docs, court fee |
| Admission | 2-4 weeks | Tribunal checks if grounds are clear and maintainable |
| Queue | 6 months to 1 year | Waiting for hearing date on tribunal calendar |
| Hearing | 1-3 hours typically | Both parties present arguments and evidence |
| Reserved Order | 1-6 months | Tribunal prepares written judgment |
| Decision | Final | ATIR order issued; reference to High Court possible |


### Tax Tribunal Lawyer in Lahore: What Is ATIR?

ATIR hears second appeals in income tax, sales tax, and Federal Excise Duty cases. The tribunal sits as a bench of two members: a judicial member and an accountant member. Established under the Income Tax Ordinance 2001 (Section 130), ATIR's [official site](https://atir.gov.pk) provides appeal forms and procedural guidance, ATIR has parallel jurisdiction under the Sales Tax Act 1990 and the Federal Excise Act 2005. The tribunal's Lahore bench handles Punjab cases; separate benches sit in Karachi, Islamabad, and Peshawar. The [FBR](https://fbr.gov.pk) maintains procedural oversight of all tribunal appeals.

### When Does Tax Tribunal in Lahore Have Jurisdiction?

#### Grounds for Appeal and Filing Deadlines

ATIR jurisdiction is triggered by appeal from the Commissioner Inland Revenue (Appeals). The Commissioner is the first appellate forum; ATIR is the second appellate tier. An appeal must be filed within 60 days of the Commissioner's order. The 60-day deadline applies across all three statutes: the Income Tax Ordinance 2001 (Section 131), the Sales Tax Act 1990 (Section 46), and the Federal Excise Act 2005 (Section 34). Late appeals can proceed only if the taxpayer obtains condonation of delay, which requires showing sufficient cause to the tribunal.

### The Chain of Tax Appeals in Pakistan

Understanding ATIR's role means understanding the full chain of forums. An income tax assessment starts with the Assessing Officer (AO), who issues an assessment order. The taxpayer may appeal to the Commissioner Inland Revenue (Appeals) within 30 days. The Commissioner decides the appeal on facts and law and issues an order. If dissatisfied, the taxpayer may appeal to ATIR within 60 days. ATIR decides on facts and law. After ATIR, only questions of law can go to the [Lahore High Court](/courts/lawyer-at-lahore-high-court) under Section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance 2001. The same structure applies to sales tax and excise disputes, with all procedures governed by the FBR.

### How Does ATIR Procedure Work in Lahore?

#### ATIR Appeal Process and Filing Steps

The process is formal and step-by-step. First, the taxpayer files a memorandum of appeal with grounds, prayer for relief, and supporting documents. Court fee is payable upfront as prescribed under tribunal rules. Next, if recovery is threatened (most often by bank account attachment), a stay application is filed. The appeal is then listed for admission hearing. At that hearing, the tribunal checks whether the grounds are clear and the appeal is maintainable. If admitted, the appeal is listed for full hearing on merits. Both parties present arguments and evidence. Finally, the tribunal issues an order. If the taxpayer wishes to challenge the order on a question of law only, a reference to the [Lahore High Court](/courts/lawyer-at-lahore-high-court) follows within 90 days.

### What Are Common ATIR Disputes?

Income tax appeals at ATIR often involve contested assessments, amendments made under Section 122 of the Ordinance, large additions on audit under Section 177, withholding tax disputes under Section 161, and wealth statement reconciliations under Section 116. Penalty appeals under Section 182 also proceed to ATIR. Sales tax cases involve input tax disallowance, valuation disputes, registration cancellation, and refund denials. Federal Excise Duty appeals concern classification of goods and exemption denials. The FBR's published position on a disputed issue affects both the Commissioner's reasoning and ATIR's approach to the matter.

### Stay of Tax Demand at ATIR

Tax recovery is a real and disruptive threat. Once an assessment order is issued, the FBR can recover the amount by attachment of bank accounts, garnishee orders on debtors, or sales of moveable property. A stay application filed at ATIR can halt this. The tribunal will grant stay during the appeal if the taxpayer demonstrates two things: hardship from immediate recovery, and a prima facie case on the merits. Hardship means real financial injury, not mere inconvenience. A prima facie case means the appeal raises serious questions that the tribunal must decide. Stay drafting is critical: vague or exaggerated hardship claims rarely succeed. A specialist tax tribunal lawyer in Lahore knows how to present hardship with precision and anchor the prima facie case in decided case law.

### How Does ATIR Differ From Commissioner Appeals?

The Commissioner Inland Revenue (Appeals) is the first appellate forum. The Commissioner reviews the Assessing Officer's order on facts and law but cannot go beyond what the AO recorded. ATIR, by contrast, is the last appellate forum where both facts and law are live issues. ATIR can examine evidence afresh and overturn the Commissioner's findings. However, ATIR does not conduct a full inquiry like the AO does. Evidence must be placed before the tribunal on paper unless the tribunal directs oral evidence. After ATIR, the Lahore High Court takes only questions of law. This matters: once ATIR has decided a factual issue, the High Court will not revisit it.

### When to Move From ATIR to the High Court

After ATIR issues its order, the only avenue remaining is the Lahore High Court, and only on questions of law. A question of law is something the court must decide in order to apply the statute correctly. If ATIR's reasoning is plainly wrong on a point of law, or if the statute itself is ambiguous, a reference to the [Lahore High Court](/courts/lawyer-at-lahore-high-court) is warranted. The reference is filed under Section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance 2001 (and parallel sections in Sales Tax Act 1990 and Federal Excise Act 2005) within 90 days of the ATIR order. Reference drafting must crystallise the legal question clearly. The High Court will not revisit facts.

### Saeed Law Firm's 50 Years of ATIR Experience

Saeed Law Firm has 50 years of specialist tax practice in Lahore, including ATIR appearances since the tribunal's establishment. Principal Bilal Saeed, Advocate (enrolled with the Punjab Bar Council), regularly appears at ATIR for income tax, sales tax, and Federal Excise Duty appeals. The firm also handles cases at the Commissioner Inland Revenue (Appeals) level and at the [Lahore High Court](/courts/lawyer-at-lahore-high-court) for references on questions of law. This coordinated practice across forums means cases are handled without strategy gaps. The office is located in Lahore's DHA Phase 3, within easy reach of all court facilities.

### Why Choose a Specialist for Your ATIR Appeal?

Each ATIR bench has preferences on evidence presentation, paper drafting, and oral argument style. Judges and accountant members expect clear, statute-specific grounds. Argument is conversational, not rhetorical. The tribunal values precise references to statute, previous tribunal decisions, and documentary evidence. A specialist tax tribunal lawyer in Lahore with 50 years' experience has seen the patterns: which arguments convince the bench, what evidence changes outcomes, how to navigate stays and recoveries. We also maintain relationships with the current judicial members and understand their reasoning patterns.

### Filing Your ATIR Appeal: What to Expect

The process begins with a detailed review of the Commissioner's order, the Assessing Officer's assessment, the taxpayer's replies, and all supporting documents. We then draft statute-specific grounds of appeal, citing relevant sections and explaining why the Commissioner's findings are wrong. We prepare the paper book: a complete bundle of all orders, replies, statements, and exhibits in sequence. If recovery is imminent, we draft and file a stay application with supporting hardship affidavit. The appeal is then listed for admission hearing, where the tribunal checks if grounds are clear and the appeal is maintainable. Once admitted, the case enters the tribunal's queue for a full hearing on merits. Hearing dates may take six months to over a year, depending on the tribunal's docket.

### Frequently Asked Questions

**How long does an ATIR appeal take from filing to decision?**
After filing, the appeal is admitted within weeks. A hearing date may take six months to over a year. The tribunal then reserves its order. Total time is typically 18 months to 3 years.

**What is the difference between Commissioner Appeals and ATIR?**
Commissioner Appeals is the first appellate forum; ATIR is the second. At both, facts and law can be argued. Beyond ATIR, only questions of law go to the Lahore High Court.

**Can I get a stay on tax recovery from ATIR?**
Yes. ATIR grants stay if you show hardship and a prima facie case on merits. Stay protects you from bank account attachment and other FBR enforcement during the appeal.

**What happens if ATIR decides against me?**
You may appeal to the Lahore High Court on questions of law only. The reference must be filed within 90 days of ATIR's order and must clearly state the legal question.

**Do I need a tax [lawyer in Lahore](/tax-lawyer-in-lahore) for ATIR appeals?**
Yes. ATIR appeals require specialist knowledge of tax statutes, procedural rules, and tribunal practice. Self-representation rarely succeeds at this appellate level.

**How much does a tax [specialist](/lawyers-in-lahore) charge for ATIR representation?**
ATIR appeals are stage-based: drafting and filing, stay application, hearing, and order. Fees are quoted upfront in writing after the initial consultation.

### Specialist Representation in ATIR: Why It Matters

Representing yourself at ATIR rarely works. The tribunal bench includes a judicial member and an accountant member, both experienced in tax matters. They expect clear grounds, properly drafted memoranda, and evidence presented in tribunal format. They appreciate oral advocates who know the statutes. A specialist tax tribunal lawyer in Lahore understands this culture. We draft grounds that are specific and statute-referenced. We prepare paper books that are organized and easy to follow. We file stay applications that are factually precise and legally sound. We make oral arguments that cite case law and statute, not rhetoric. This specialist approach increases the likelihood of admission and of success on the merits. The 50 years of Saeed Law Firm's practice in ATIR appeals reflects this commitment to specialist excellence.

### How Saeed Law Firm Handles Your ATIR Appeal

When a client comes to our office with an ATIR appeal, we begin with a thorough review of the entire file. We obtain copies of the Assessing Officer's assessment order, the Commissioner Inland Revenue (Appeals) order, all replies filed by the taxpayer, and all supporting documentary evidence. We review the grounds of rejection to understand exactly why the Commissioner decided against the taxpayer. We then strategize. Which aspects of the Commissioner's order are most vulnerable to challenge? What evidence in the file supports reversal? Where are the gaps that we need to address through further documentation or witness evidence? Once we understand the case fully, we draft statute-specific grounds of appeal. Each ground must be clear, specific, and tied to a section of the relevant tax statute. Vague grounds do not survive admission; precise grounds do. We then prepare the paper book, which is the complete record of the case organized in the order required by tribunal procedure. The paper book makes it easy for the tribunal to follow our argument: here is the assessment order, here is the Commissioner's order, here are the taxpayer's replies, here is the evidence. If recovery is imminent, we prepare a detailed stay application with supporting hardship affidavit. The affidavit explains the financial impact of immediate recovery and supports it with facts. We then file everything with the tribunal within the 60-day deadline. The appeal is listed for admission hearing, where the tribunal checks if the grounds are clear and the appeal is maintainable. At that hearing, we present the appeal succinctly. If the tribunal admits the appeal, it is later listed for full hearing on merits. We then coordinate witness testimony if needed, prepare written submissions on facts and law, and appear for oral argument. We make the argument conversational, not rhetorical, and we cite statute and case law throughout. When the tribunal issues its order, we review it immediately. If we believe the tribunal got a question of law wrong, we advise the client on a reference to the Lahore High Court. We draft and file that reference within 90 days if the client wishes to proceed. Throughout this entire process, we keep the client informed at each stage. We explain what to expect, what evidence we need, and what the likely outcomes are. We answer questions. We deliver candid advice, not optimistic fiction. This is how a specialist tax tribunal lawyer in Lahore operates: with care, precision, and honest judgment.

### The FBR's Role in ATIR Appeals

The Federal Board of Revenue (FBR) through the Commissioner or the Commissioner's office presents the government's case at ATIR. The FBR's counsel will argue that the Commissioner's order was correct, that the assessment was lawful, and that the taxpayer's appeal should be dismissed. Understanding the FBR's typical arguments and counter-arguments is part of specialist practice. We anticipate the government's position and prepare our clients' case with those arguments in mind. The FBR maintains oversight of all inland revenue matters through its administrative structure and published policy guidance. Our practice includes regular monitoring of FBR clarifications and position statements so we advise clients based on the latest government stance.

faqs.q: How long does an ATIR appeal take from filing to decision?
faqs.a: After filing, the appeal is admitted within weeks. A hearing date may take six months to over a year. The tribunal then reserves its order. Total time is typically 18 months to 3 years from filing to final decision.

faqs.q: What is the difference between Commissioner Appeals and ATIR?
faqs.a: Commissioner Appeals is the first appellate forum. ATIR is the second and final stage where facts and law can be argued. Beyond ATIR, only questions of law go to the Lahore High Court. Both forums review on facts and law.

faqs.q: Can I get a stay on tax recovery from ATIR?
faqs.a: Yes. ATIR grants stay if you demonstrate hardship from immediate recovery and a prima facie case on the merits. Stay protects you from bank account attachment and other FBR enforcement action during the appeal.

faqs.q: What happens if ATIR decides against me?
faqs.a: You may appeal to the Lahore High Court, but only on questions of law. The reference must be filed within 90 days of ATIR's order. The High Court will not re-examine facts, only the legal reasoning and application of statute.

faqs.q: How do I book a consultation with Saeed Law Firm?
faqs.a: Use the contact form on this site, call our Lahore office at +92-319-4959420, or message us on WhatsApp. We respond within one business day and schedule a consultation appointment for most matters within the same week. Your initial consultation is normally PKR 8,000, free for a limited time.

faqs.q: Do you handle references to the Lahore High Court after ATIR?
faqs.a: Yes. Bilal Saeed, Advocate (Punjab Bar Council), practices at the Lahore High Court and handles references on questions of law from ATIR orders. We also appear at the Commissioner Inland Revenue (Appeals) level.
